reasoning: Border closure by Benin on May 6-7, 2025 involves serious dual issues: terrorism concerns (Mahmuda group + arms smuggling) and trade policy (soya/cashew export restrictions). While Nigerian House of Representatives took urgent action on May 13-14 urging diplomatic resolution, historical precedent shows Nigeria-Benin border closures last months/years, not weeks. The closure was ordered by President Talon himself and no diplomatic progress has been reported as of May 14. Combined with only a 55-day window until July 1 resolution date and no evidence of active negotiations, the likelihood of reopening within this timeframe is low.
